blockingIssues.description: `derive_filtered_input_source_indices()` still reconstructs filtered-item provenance after `call_model_input_filter` returns using identity, fingerprint, sibling-order, and last-remaining fallbacks, so multi-item fresh replacements after drops/reorders can still mark the wrong prepared source as sent and replay a delivered delta later.

rejectedObservations.summary: Accepted narrow omission for ambiguous multi-item fresh-replacement provenance under call_model_input_filter.
rejectedObservations.justification: Full Python-style source attribution for server-managed conversation deltas is not safely achievable under the current Rust call_model_input_filter API when the filter drops or reorders siblings and returns multiple fresh replacement items, because ModelInputData carries no provenance-aware channel. The mission preserves the clean public API and will document this narrow omission explicitly in the final parity ledger refresh.
